hints.text: Very **mountainous, fjords** along most of the coast (Finland/Denmark flat; Sweden mixed)
Mostly **wooden houses**; many **red or black-painted**; Norway has more **white** houses & fewer brick than SE/FI
Long white plate + EU strip; **green commercial plates** common on vans (green plates most-likely Norway in Europe)
Letters **Æ Ø Å** (Danish also; Swedish only Å); more E's than Swedish; Norwegian **kk** vs Swedish **ck**
Small roads = **long dashed white outer lines**; big roads solid + **orange-tinged yellow** middle; lines often dotted
Bollard: thin curved rectangle, reflector inside a **black parallelogram**
**Snow poles orange**, thin white reflector **mid-pole** (Sweden wider/higher; Finland at very top); wooden ones w/ painted reflectors
Guardrails often on **wooden posts** (not common in SE/FI)
Pedestrian sign: **4 stripes**, simply-drawn person (Sweden detailed no hat; Finland 5 stripes)
Road signs = **red outline + white background** (Sweden & Finland = yellow); chevrons yellow-on-black
Directional signs **yellow + black border**; road # in green box (Euro/major) or white (minor); blue **M** signs on small roads
Wooden poles w/ small **black metal cap**; lamp mounted w/ **single bolt** (Sweden/Finland double)
Traditional houses often have **grass-covered roofs** (far rarer in SE/FI)
Street suffixes **-veien / -gata / -gate**; street signs **centred on top** of the post (Sweden = side/lamp)
Road number **first digit = region**; small white signs show road # (prefix **R** = findable, **F**/Fv = hard) + distance
**Sámi bilingual signs** in the north — letters **Á Đ** and many vowels in a row
Deep **fjords + tall mountains** between Bergen–Trondheim; sharp rocky treeless peaks on **Lofoten**
Farmlands in 3 zones: **SE/Oslo** (flat, rolling forested hills), **Trondheim** (steep hilly), **Stavanger** (vast open, round rock walls, grass)
**Black snow poles** most common between Bergen and Trondheim
**Oslo**: fairly large black or red pole plates; SE Norway = small wide black plates
**Stavanger**: two **red stripes** around lamp posts
**Bergen**: distinctive green **fire hydrants**
**Russian border** (Kirkenes, E6/E105): humid green foggy road, thin birches/pines; Russian text on signs
**Røros**: densely-built traditional wooden mining town
